rows.locator: CCME CWQG for Aquatic Life: Halogenated Methanes (Trichloromethane), Table 1 (freshwater interim guideline)
rows.source_excerpt: The interim water quality guideline for trichloromethane for the protection of freshwater life is 1.8 ug/L. It was derived by multiplying the LOEC of 18 ug/L for teratogenesis in spring peepers by a safety factor of 0.1 (CCME 1991, 1992).
rows.note: CCME interim CWQG 1.8 ug/L freshwater aquatic-life (trichloromethane, CAS 67-66-3); value + derivation verified 2026-06-19 against the CCME factsheet PDF (https://ccme.ca/en/res/halogenated-methanes-trichloromethane-en-canadian-water-quality-guidelines-for-the-protection-of-aquatic-life.pdf; compiled in the 1999 CEQG). Attribute to CCME only -- EPA NRWQC has no chloroform aquatic-life CCC.
